opp.ais_why:
• Per the 4 May 2026 rep note from Carl Kimball: 'no engagement to speak of- ck to drive CIO meeting F2F in May timeframe.' Per the (undated) rep note: 'Ariel shared that a new inter-SBU project is launching next week, requiring a procurement platform. The project is collaborative across SBUs and will involve the retail group as the first onboarded supplier. Zycus remains the preferred platform, consistent with evaluations since 2019.' Ariel Faraon is the named contact - but his quoted language is about org structure and SBU onboarding, never AI.
• Per the 21 [Mar?] rep note: 'Working with Charlie to elevate project evaluation process to CTO (Tina)- Issues are arising to get a group wide consensus at Ariel level. ck to work with Luc (when onboard) to elevate org structure. Individual organizations are contacting Zycus to "go it alone", including Gotyme and Cebu Air.' Product scope is the bare minimum: eProcurement only - no Merlin, no Agentic AI, no ANA.
• Medium confidence: NextStep is rich but exclusively about org politics/SBU consensus, not AI. The single Avoma meeting fuzzy-matched to Gamuda Land (gamudaland.com.my) so no JG Summit buyer transcript. Product list is just 'eProcurement' - the only single-product opp in the batch and devoid of AI. Clear AI Resistant on absence.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (undated): 'stronger business case due to group-wide and retail involvement' (relative, not quantified)
Avoma 21 Apr 2025 (Horizon panel): Ariel Faraon 'one-click procurement by 2028,' 'transactional to strategic,' 'governance is a major challenge' (qualitative transformation goals, no numbers)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI has been articulated by the buyer across any channel. The Next Step log references 'a stronger business case due to group-wide and retail involvement' but this is a relative statement (stronger than prior evaluations) with no numbers. The undated Next Step notes that 'a new inter-SBU project is launching next week, requiring a procurement platform' and that the 'project is collaborative across SBUs and will involve the retail group as the first onboarded supplier,' but no cost, cycle-time, headcount, or savings target is named. The single Avoma call (21 Apr 2025, Horizon panel dry run) was an internal Zycus event-prep session with the relevant stakeholder as a panelist; Ariel spoke about JG Summit's transformation goals in the abstract ('one-click procurement by 2028,' 'elevate procurement from transactional to strategic,' 'governance is a major challenge'), but no quantified pain or value case was stated. The opportunity remains in Qualified for 150 days with no documented value case, which is a foundational gap. The recommended move is to co-build a quantified business case with the Decision Maker and the finance/CFO stakeholder (named in Next Step as a required escalation point) that ties platform ROI to group-wide SBU adoption and retail supplier onboarding.

champion.narrative: Ariel Francisco Faraon (Director of Procurement, ariel.faraon@jgsummit.com.ph) is the sole identified contact and is marked Decision Maker in OpportunityContactRole. He has been engaged since at least 13 Mar 2026 (Next Step: 'ck reached out to Ariel and Karla for connect and status check') and participated as a panelist in the Zycus Horizon SEA 2025 event (21 Apr 2025 Avoma call), where he publicly advocated for Zycus as the preferred platform ('Zycus remains the preferred platform, consistent with evaluations since 2019,' Next Step undated). On the panel, Ariel articulated detailed transformation criteria aligned to Zycus capabilities ('partnership fit, governance built in, AI that can initiate/prioritize/act, Agentic AI as co-pilot') and stated 'we need a partner like Zycus, a partner that can help us through this transformation journey' (Avoma 21 Apr 2025). This is strong public advocacy. However, Ariel's influence is limited: the Next Step log states 'Issues are arising to get a group wide consensus at Ariel level' (undated ~Feb 2026) and 'no engagement with the relevant stakeholder group' (26 May 2026), indicating Ariel cannot advance the deal without group-level sponsorship. His engagement has cooled: last activity 13 May 2026 (44 days ago), and the 26 May Next Step rates the deal '25% Confidence' and 'on hold.' Ariel has not introduced Zycus to the CTO, CFO, or group-level sponsor despite multiple Next Step entries requesting escalation (31 Mar, 13 Mar). His champion strength is developing to weak: he advocates for Zycus publicly and owns the pain, but lacks the power to drive group-level approval and has not facilitated upward access. The recommended move is to re-engage Ariel with a specific request: co-author a group-level business case and secure a CFO or group sponsor intro within 14 days, or clarify whether the SBU-by-SBU path (Gotyme, Cebu Air) is the faster route and pivot accordingly.

sources: SF Competitors__c: GEP Worldwide (no date, no context)

competition.narrative: GEP Worldwide is the sole named competitor, listed in the Salesforce Competitors__c field with no additional context, date, or quote. No other source (Next Step, task, or Avoma call) mentions GEP, any other competitor, or the incumbent solution being replaced. The Next Step log states 'Zycus remains the preferred platform, consistent with evaluations since 2019' (undated), suggesting Zycus was selected or shortlisted in a prior RFP round, but the current competitive landscape (whether GEP is still active, whether new entrants are in play, whether the 2019 evaluation included GEP) is unknown. The absence of recent competitive intelligence (no call, no buyer quote, no competitive review in the 150-day Qualified stage) is a gap. The threat level of GEP is unknown but treated as dormant given the lack of any mention in 2026 activity. The recommended move is to surface the current competitive landscape in the re-engagement conversation with the Decision Maker: ask directly whether GEP or any other vendor is being evaluated alongside Zycus for the inter-SBU project, and whether the 2019 evaluation shortlist is still valid or requires refresh.

SF Next_Step__c (undated): 'Individual organizations contacting Zycus to go it alone'

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is articulated at a strategic level but not operationalized or quantified. On the Avoma panel call (21 Apr 2025), Ariel Faraon described JG Summit's procurement challenges: 'governance is a major challenge,' 'we are a diverse conglomerate in different industries,' 'our people are too focused on transactional tasks, 80% of time on transactional documents,' 'we cannot extract value, cannot look for cost-saving opportunities, people don't have time to upgrade themselves,' 'we want to elevate procurement from transactional to strategic,' and 'we don't want our people to be taken over by AI but to control AI and increase their value as professionals.' These are real, articulated pain points owned by Ariel (Director of Procurement). The Next Step log adds operational pain: 'Individual organizations are contacting Zycus to go it alone' (fragmentation, lack of group-wide platform control), and 'Issues are arising to get a group wide consensus at Ariel level' (governance and alignment pain). However, no cost of the current pain (manual effort hours, compliance risk incidents, missed savings, cycle-time delays) is quantified. The pain owner is Ariel at the procurement-practice level, but the business pain owner at the group/CFO level is not engaged. The recommended move is to translate Ariel's articulated pain into a quantified business case (cost of fragmentation, savings from group-wide platform, productivity gain from transactional automation) and present it to the CFO/group sponsor to convert strategic pain into commercial urgency.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, procurement channel, InfoSec, or implementation mechanics are documented. No MSA, SOW, Order Form, redline, legal review, or procurement approval workflow has been mentioned in any Next Step note, task, or call. No partner or SI involvement is noted. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c or renewal forcing function is present. The deal is stalled at the group-level strategic hold (26 May 2026) with no forward movement into commercial or contracting stages. The paper process is entirely unknown. The recommended move is to surface the paper process requirements (MSA/SOW template, legal review cycle, InfoSec questionnaire, group-wide approval thresholds) once the group-level sponsor is engaged and the decision to proceed (group-wide vs SBU-by-SBU) is clarified.

economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer is not identified. The Next Step log names the CFO as a required escalation ('checking my previous contacts from JG Summit if they can connect me to the CIO or CFO,' 31 Mar 2026), and notes that 'Issues are arising to get a group wide consensus at Ariel level' (undated, ~Feb 2026), signaling that budget authority sits above Ariel Faraon (Director of Procurement, the sole OpportunityContactRole marked Decision Maker). No CFO or C-level executive is in the contact-role roster, no CFO has appeared on a buyer call, and no commercial or pricing conversation is logged. The 26 May Next Step states the project is 'on hold' at the 'JG Holding group' level, confirming that the economic decision sits at the group/parent level, not with the relevant stakeholder panel call (21 Apr 2025) featured Ariel as a speaker but was an internal Zycus event-prep session with no buyer commercial discussion. The absence of EB engagement after 150 days and the stated hold at group level are the primary blockers. The recommended move is to secure an introduction to the CFO or group-level sponsor through Ariel or another internal champion network contact (Karla Borromeo, Lisa Gokongwei-Cheng) and schedule a value/ROI discussion anchored to the group-wide business case.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is visibly complex and stalled. The Next Step log reveals a multi-layer approval chain: Ariel Faraon (Director of Procurement, the sole contact role) cannot advance the deal alone ('Issues are arising to get a group wide consensus at Ariel level,' undated ~Feb 2026). The process requires escalation to the CTO ('Working with Charlie to elevate project evaluation process to CTO (Tina),' undated ~Feb 2026), then to the CFO ('checking my previous contacts from JG Summit if they can connect me to the CIO or CFO,' 31 Mar 2026), and ultimately to the 'JG Holding group' parent level (26 May 2026: 'no engagement with the relevant stakeholder group- this project is on hold'). The log also notes SBU fragmentation risk: 'Individual organizations are contacting Zycus to go it alone, including Gotyme and Cebu Air' (undated), signaling that the group-wide decision is being undermined or bypassed by subsidiary-level initiatives. No approval sequence, timeline, or formal gate structure is documented. The single Avoma call (21 Apr 2025) was an internal Zycus panel-prep session, not a buyer-side decision-process meeting. The current state (26 May) is 'on hold' at the group level with no engagement, and the deal team is pivoting to 'engaging directly with group companies' (26 May). The recommended move is to map the actual decision structure (Ariel → CTO → CFO → JG Holding group sponsor) in writing, secure a group-level sponsor intro, and clarify whether the path forward is group-wide platform or SBU-by-SBU adoption, then align the process and timeline accordingly.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (undated): 'Zycus remains the preferred platform, consistent with evaluations since 2019'

Avoma 21 Apr 2025 (panel): Ariel Faraon 'partnership fit, governance built in, AI initiate/prioritize/act, capability building, Agentic AI empowers people' (strategic criteria, public panel, not deal-specific RFP)
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are partially visible but not formalized. The Next Step log states that 'Zycus remains the preferred platform, consistent with evaluations since 2019' (undated), indicating prior RFP or evaluation rounds where Zycus was shortlisted or selected, but no current evaluation framework, scorecard, or RFP is referenced. The stated requirement is 'a procurement platform' for 'a new inter-SBU project' that is 'collaborative across SBUs' and will 'involve the retail group as the first onboarded supplier' (Next Step, undated). On the Avoma panel call (21 Apr 2025), Ariel Faraon articulated JG Summit's transformation priorities: 'partnership fit with a purpose,' 'governance built in, not bolted on,' 'AI that can initiate, prioritize, and act while respecting thresholds, workflows, and controls,' 'solution must support capability building, change adoption, and leadership alignment,' and 'Agentic AI must empower people, not bypass them.' These are strategic criteria but were stated in a public panel context, not a formal RFP or buyer-side evaluation session. The gap is that no current, deal-specific evaluation criteria, test cases, or SBU-level fit requirements have been documented in 2026. The recommended move is to run a fit workshop with Ariel and the SBU leads (Gotyme, Cebu Air named in Next Step as organizations 'contacting Zycus to go it alone') to translate the 2019 evaluation and the 2025 panel criteria into a 2026 group-wide scorecard aligned to the inter-SBU project scope.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit signal is AI Curious with strong appetite articulated but not operationalized. The Decision Maker (Ariel Faraon) articulated detailed Agentic AI appetite on the Horizon panel (21 Apr 2025): 'we want to prioritize AI now or we will be left behind,' 'AI that can initiate, prioritize, and act,' 'Agentic AI as co-pilot to elevate procurement from transactional to strategic,' 'one-click procurement by 2028.' This is AI Hungry language. However, the product scope is 'eProcurement' only (no Merlin, no Agentic AI, no AI/ML module), and no AI-specific activity (demo, POC, ROI workshop, AI use-case validation) has occurred in the 150-day deal cycle. The Next Step log is silent on AI in all 2026 entries. The AIS fields are null (no AIS score or status in Salesforce). The gap is that the articulated AI appetite (Hungry on the panel, Apr 2025) has not translated into deal-specific AI engagement or product scope in 2026, so the current operational signal is Curious (aware, interested, but not yet acting). The recommended move is to operationalize the AI appetite by anchoring the re-engagement to the Agentic AI vision Ariel articulated, co-build an AI-led business case, and position AI as the solution to the group-wide governance and transactional-overhead pain.

deal_movement.summary: Created 27 Jan 2026, entered Qualified immediately, and has remained in Qualified for 150 days with no stage progression. The Next Step log shows escalating stall signals: 13 Mar owner reached out to Decision Maker and another contact for status check; 31 Mar owner began internal-network attempts to reach CIO or CFO; 4 May owner noted 'no engagement to speak of'; 26 May owner noted 'project is on hold' at JG Holding group level and deal team is pivoting to 'engaging directly with group companies' (SBU-by-SBU). Last verified activity 13 May 2026 (44 days ago). No buyer call, no demo, no RFP, no commercial discussion, no progression milestone in 150 days. The single Avoma meeting (21 Apr 2025, before opp creation) was a Zycus internal Horizon panel dry-run with the Decision Maker as a panelist, not a buyer-side deal conversation. Movement is stalled at group-level strategic hold with SBU fragmentation risk.

items.detail: Group-level hold with no Economic Buyer or C-level sponsor engagement. Deal is 'on hold' at JG Holding group level (26 May 2026) with 'no engagement' for 44 days. CFO and group sponsor not identified or reached. Ariel (Director of Procurement, the sole contact) cannot advance the deal without group-level buy-in ('Issues arising at Ariel level,' Next Step undated ~Feb 2026). Close date 30 Oct 2026 (126 days out) is non-credible given 150-day stall in Qualified and stated hold.

SBU fragmentation risk: individual business units (Gotyme, Cebu Air named in Next Step undated ~Feb 2026) are 'contacting Zycus to go it alone,' bypassing the group-wide platform decision. This may collapse the $150k group deal into smaller SBU deals or no deal, or may fragment the buyer org and eliminate the group-level sponsor path entirely.

Single-threaded champion at risk: deal is single-threaded to the relevant stakeholder (Director of Procurement), who lacks group-level power and has not facilitated upward access to CTO or CFO despite multiple requests. Engagement cooling: last contact 13 May 2026 (44 days ago), deal rated 25% confidence (26 May 2026). High ghost risk if Ariel is bypassed or disengages.

No Economic Buyer identified, no budget approval path documented, no commercial or pricing discussion in 150 days. Amount $150k is placeholder-grade (round number, no line-item pricing). Budget authority sits at CFO or group level per Next Step (31 Mar: 'checking contacts for CIO or CFO'), but no CFO engagement.

champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (Decision Maker), Avoma 21 Apr 2025 (panel), SF Next_Step__c 13 Mar / 31 Mar / 26 May 2026, LastActivityDate 13 May 2026
champion_strength.summary: Ariel Francisco Faraon (Director of Procurement) is the sole engaged contact and demonstrates developing-to-weak champion strength. He publicly advocated for Zycus on the Horizon panel (21 Apr 2025), articulated detailed criteria aligned to Zycus capabilities, and stated 'we need a partner like Zycus.' However, he lacks group-level power to advance the deal ('Issues arising at Ariel level,' Next Step undated ~Feb 2026), has not facilitated upward access to CTO or CFO despite multiple requests (31 Mar, 13 Mar), and the deal is now 'on hold' at the JG Holding group level with 'no engagement' (26 May 2026). Engagement is cooling: last contact 13 May 2026 (44 days ago), deal rated 25% confidence. At risk: may be bypassed if SBU organizations (Gotyme, Cebu Air) go alone, and the group-wide decision path he represents is stalled. The recommended move is to re-engage Ariel with a specific request (co-author group business case and secure CFO intro within 14 days) or pivot to SBU-by-SBU strategy if group path remains blocked.

items.action: Re-engage the Decision Maker with CFO intro request and group business-case co-build within 7 days.

items.expected_effect: Restart buyer engagement, clarify whether group-wide path is viable or SBU-by-SBU pivot required, secure CFO or C-level sponsor intro to break group-level hold and advance deal from 150-day Qualified stall. Without this re-engagement in next 7 days, deal moves to dark/disqualify by mid-July.

Group-level sponsor (CFO) will require quantified case to advance from hold.
items.expected_effect: Convert strategic pain into commercial urgency, arm Decision Maker with CFO-ready business case (cost of SBU fragmentation, savings from group-wide platform, productivity gain from transactional automation), create group-level sponsor engagement path, and justify $150k investment to break hold and advance to commercial stage.

Ariel has not facilitated upward access and is now unresponsive (44 days). High ghost risk if single-threaded contact disengages.
items.expected_effect: Build parallel sponsorship paths, reduce dependency on single contact who lacks group-level power, reach decision-makers and influencers directly (CTO for technical validation, Karla for strategic categories alignment, SBU leads for land-and-expand path), and reduce ghost risk by creating multiple active buyer threads.

items.action: Clarify group-wide vs SBU-by-SBU path and pivot strategy accordingly within 14 days.

Path forward is unclear: group-wide platform or SBU-by-SBU land-and-expand. Decision Maker (Ariel) represents group path but is stalled; SBU organizations are inbound.
items.expected_effect: If group-wide path remains blocked after re-engagement, pivot to SBU-by-SBU land-and-expand: engage Gotyme and Cebu Air directly (already inbound), land one SBU quickly as reference (smaller deal, faster cycle), then use SBU success to pull group-wide adoption. This converts fragmentation threat into land-and-expand wedge and creates momentum while group decision is stalled, preserving deal value and buyer relationship.

Next Step log silent on AI in all 2026 entries. AI appetite articulated but not operationalized.
items.expected_effect: Operationalize the AI appetite by co-building an AI-led business case (e.g. Merlin negotiation agent for supplier price optimization across SBUs, Agentic intake for inter-SBU collaboration), position AI as the solution to the group-wide governance and transactional-overhead pain Ariel named, differentiate Zycus from GEP (dormant competitor) on Agentic AI maturity, and align product scope to the transformation vision Ariel publicly advocated, raising deal value and strategic fit.

confidence_signals.summary: Confidence is cooling and deal is at high ghost risk. Last verified activity 13 May 2026 (44 days ago), no buyer call or email since. The 26 May Next Step rates the deal '25% Confidence' and states 'no engagement with JG Holding group, this project is on hold.' Prior Next Step entries show escalating stall: 4 May 'no engagement to speak of,' 31 Mar owner attempting internal-network restart to reach CIO/CFO, 13 Mar owner reaching out for status check. The single Avoma meeting (21 Apr 2025) was a Zycus internal Horizon panel prep session before opp creation, not a buyer-side deal conversation. No demo, no RFP, no commercial discussion, no progression milestone in 150-day Qualified stage. The deal team is pivoting to 'engaging directly with group companies' (SBU-by-SBU, 26 May), signaling that the group-wide path is blocked. Cooling signals: 44-day silence, stated hold, pivoting strategy, single-threaded contact who has gone quiet.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 27 Jan 2026, 150 days old. Stage Qualified since creation, 150 days in stage with no progression. Close date 30 Oct 2026, 126 days out. Last activity 13 May 2026, 44 days ago. Next Step log shows escalating hold signals: 26 May noted project on hold at group level, 4 May noted no engagement, 31 Mar noted internal network restart attempts. No submission-date series data; no measurable stage velocity. Forward slip not yet recorded but close date is increasingly non-credible given 150-day stall in Qualified and stated group-level hold.

north_star_verdict.headline: Group-level project remains on hold with zero buyer engagement for 44 days and 150 days stalled in Qualified stage; single-threaded contact network, SBUs bypassing group to engage vendor directly, no consensus or revival signal, group procurement initiative effectively shelved.

best_practice_check.summary: Three substantive win-strategy levers for this deal: (1) Break the group-level hold by securing CFO or C-level sponsor intro and co-building a quantified group-wide business case tied to SBU adoption and retail onboarding, converting strategic pain into commercial urgency. (2) Clarify the path forward (group-wide platform vs SBU-by-SBU land-and-expand) and align the approach accordingly; if group path remains blocked, pivot to the SBU organizations already reaching out (Gotyme, Cebu Air) and land one as reference to pull group adoption. (3) Multi-thread aggressively: the deal is single-threaded to one contact (Ariel) who lacks group-level power; add the CTO, Karla Borromeo, and SBU leads to the stakeholder map and engage them directly to build parallel sponsorship and reduce ghost risk.

competitive_position.summary: GEP Worldwide is the sole named competitor (Salesforce Competitors__c) but is dormant in the current cycle: no mention in any 2026 Next Step note, task, or buyer conversation, and no competitive activity or quote in 150 days. The Next Step log states 'Zycus remains the preferred platform, consistent with evaluations since 2019' (undated), indicating Zycus was selected or shortlisted in a prior RFP round, but the current competitive status of GEP (whether still in consideration, whether eliminated, whether threat level) is unknown. No other competitor, incumbent vendor, or do-nothing alternative is named. The single strongest current threat is not GEP but the do-nothing / hold status: the deal is 'on hold' at the JG Holding group level (26 May 2026) with 'no engagement,' and SBU fragmentation ('Individual organizations are contacting Zycus to go it alone') may bypass the group-wide platform decision entirely, collapsing the $150k group deal into smaller SBU deals or no deal. The competitive wedge is group-wide governance and SBU collaboration, which Zycus positioning ('partnership fit, governance built in, Agentic AI empowers people') directly addresses per the Decision Maker's public advocacy (Avoma 21 Apr 2025).

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is partially articulated but not operationalized or validated in this deal cycle. The Decision Maker (Ariel Faraon) articulated strong AI appetite and detailed Agentic AI criteria on the Horizon panel (21 Apr 2025): 'AI that can initiate, prioritize, and act,' 'Agentic AI must empower people, not bypass them,' 'AI as trusted co-pilot to rise from tactical execution to strategic orchestration,' and 'we want to prioritize AI now or we will be left behind by our competition.' However, the product scope is 'eProcurement' only (Salesforce Products__c) with no Merlin, Agentic AI, or AI/ML module listed, and no AI-specific demo, ROI workshop, or use-case validation has occurred in the 150-day Qualified stage. The Next Step log focuses exclusively on org politics, SBU consensus, and group-level escalation, with no mention of AI positioning or differentiation in any 2026 activity. The gap is that the AI narrative articulated in the public panel (pre-deal, Apr 2025) has not translated into deal-specific AI scope, AI business case, or AI-led engagement in 2026. The recommended move is to anchor the re-engagement conversation to the Agentic AI vision Ariel articulated on the panel, co-build an AI-led business case (e.g. Merlin negotiation agent for supplier price optimization, Agentic intake for SBU collaboration), and position AI as the wedge to solve the group-wide governance pain he named.

items.quote: Our first nonnegotiable is the partnership fit. Partnership fit with a purpose. We need a tech partner who understands that rewiring is not just a tech upgrade, but a cultural shift. We need a partner like Zycus, a partner that can really help us through this transformation journey.
items.criterion: Partnership fit with a purpose (partner who understands rewiring as cultural shift, supports capability building, change adoption, leadership alignment)

items.quote: Solution must have governance built in, not bolted on. We are operating in a complex multi-business environment, different industries. We need AI that can initiate, prioritize, and act while respecting thresholds, workflows, and controls that keep procurement compliant, agile, and audit ready.
items.criterion: Governance built in, not bolted on (AI that respects thresholds, workflows, controls; keeps procurement compliant, agile, audit-ready)

items.quote: Agentic AI must empower people, not bypass them. When AI becomes a trusted co-pilot, not just a tool, it enables procurement teams to rise from tactical execution to strategic orchestration. That's the kind of transformation we're after.
items.criterion: Agentic AI must empower people, not bypass them (AI as trusted co-pilot, enables procurement teams to rise from tactical execution to strategic orchestration)

items.quote: Issues are arising to get a group wide consensus at Ariel level. Individual organizations are contacting Zycus to go it alone, including Gotyme and Cebu Air.
items.criterion: Group-wide consensus and governance (multi-SBU alignment, single platform across diverse conglomerate)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Partial fit visibility. The Decision Maker (Ariel Faraon) articulated detailed transformation criteria on the Avoma panel call (21 Apr 2025) that align strongly to Zycus platform capabilities: 'partnership fit with a purpose' (Zycus partner model), 'governance built in not bolted on' (Zycus multi-entity governance), 'AI that can initiate, prioritize, and act while respecting thresholds, workflows, and controls' (Agentic AI positioning), 'solution must support capability building, change adoption, and leadership alignment' (Zycus services and change management), and 'Agentic AI must empower people, not bypass them' (Zycus co-pilot narrative). Ariel stated 'we need a partner like Zycus' on the panel. However, these criteria were stated in a public event context, not a formal RFP or deal-specific evaluation session, and no deal-specific fit validation (demo, POC, fit workshop, scorecard) has occurred in the 150-day Qualified stage. The exposure is group-wide governance and SBU consensus: the Next Step log states 'Issues are arising to get a group wide consensus at Ariel level' (undated ~Feb 2026) and the project is 'on hold' at the group level (26 May 2026), indicating that Ariel's fit assessment has not translated to group-level buy-in. The recommended move is to run a group-wide fit workshop with Ariel, SBU leads (Gotyme, Cebu Air), and the CTO or CFO to validate the 2025 panel criteria against the 2026 inter-SBU project scope and translate fit into group-level sponsorship.

hard.ais_why:
• Per the 4 May 2026 rep note from Carl Kimball: 'no engagement to speak of- ck to drive CIO meeting F2F in May timeframe.' Per the (undated) rep note: 'Ariel shared that a new inter-SBU project is launching next week, requiring a procurement platform. The project is collaborative across SBUs and will involve the retail group as the first onboarded supplier. Zycus remains the preferred platform, consistent with evaluations since 2019.' Ariel Faraon is the named contact - but his quoted language is about org structure and SBU onboarding, never AI.
• Per the 21 [Mar?] rep note: 'Working with Charlie to elevate project evaluation process to CTO (Tina)- Issues are arising to get a group wide consensus at Ariel level. ck to work with Luc (when onboard) to elevate org structure. Individual organizations are contacting Zycus to "go it alone", including Gotyme and Cebu Air.' Product scope is the bare minimum: eProcurement only - no Merlin, no Agentic AI, no ANA.
• Medium confidence: NextStep is rich but exclusively about org politics/SBU consensus, not AI. The single Avoma meeting fuzzy-matched to Gamuda Land (gamudaland.com.my) so no JG Summit buyer transcript. Product list is just 'eProcurement' - the only single-product opp in the batch and devoid of AI. Clear AI Resistant on absence.

This converts the fragmentation threat into a land-and-expand opportunity and creates momentum while the group-level decision is stalled.

gaps: Single Avoma call (21 Apr 2025) was a Zycus internal Horizon panel dry-run session with Decision Maker as panelist, not a buyer-side deal conversation for this opportunity (JG Summit-2026 created 27 Jan 2026, after the call). No buyer-side discovery, demo, RFP, or commercial call has been recorded for this deal in 150 days.
No OpportunityLineItem pricing data (product scope is 'eProcurement' string only, no line-item breakdown, no Merlin/AI modules).
AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are null in Salesforce; no AI excitement score or status recorded.

No Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, or Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c in Salesforce (stage-date series fields not populated); true time-in-stage cannot be computed from submission dates.
No Current_Contract_Expiration__c or renewal forcing function.
No Tasks or Events in 90d window returned from Salesforce query (LastActivityDate 13 May 2026 is the only date anchor).
Next Step log entries (13 Mar, 31 Mar, 4 May, 26 May) are dated but several entries are undated (treating as ~Jan-Feb 2026 based on context); exact dates for some movements are inferred.

validation gate: hard.ais_why '• Per the 4 May 2026 rep note from Carl Kimball: \'no engagement to speak of- ck to drive CIO meeting F2F in May timeframe.\' Per the (undated) rep note: \'Ariel shared that a new inter-SBU project is launching next week, requiring a procurement platform. The project is collaborative across SBUs and will involve the retail group as the first onboarded supplier. Zycus remains the preferred platform, consistent with evaluations since 2019.\' Ariel Faraon is the named contact - but his quoted language is about org structure and SBU onboarding, never AI. • Per the 21 rep note: \'Working with Charlie to elevate project evaluation process to CTO (Tina)- Issues are arising to get a group wide consensus at Ariel level. ck to work with Luc (when onboard) to elevate org structure. Individual organizations are contacting Zycus to "go it alone", including Gotyme and Cebu Air.\' Product scope is the bare minimum: eProcurement only - no Merlin, no Agentic AI, no ANA. • Medium confidence: NextStep is rich but exclusively about org politics/SBU consensus, not AI. The single Avoma meeting fuzzy-matched to Gamuda Land (gamudaland.com.my) so no JG Summit buyer transcript. Product list is just \'eProcurement\' - the only single-product opp in the batch and devoid of AI.
